personal.name: Mahammad Siraj Cheruvu
personal.title: Software Engineer · AI Engineer · Data Engineer
personal.tagline: I build production software, design intelligent AI systems, and engineer scalable data pipelines, all the way from prototype to production.

about: I'm a graduate student in AI and Business Analytics at USF (GPA 3.88) who operates across three domains: I engineer reliable software - REST APIs, clean backend architecture, and full-stack features; I build AI systems - multi-agent LLM pipelines, RAG frameworks, and production-grade ML workflows; and I design data infrastructure - real-time Kafka/Spark ETL pipelines, analytics dashboards, and scalable storage solutions. My goal is always the same: turn complex problems into working, production-quality systems.

education.degree: Masters in Artificial Intelligence and Business Analytics
B.Tech in Computer Science and Engineering

bullets: Collected, cleaned, and maintained departmental data to build Tableau and Power BI analytics dashboards, enabling data-driven reporting for university staff.
Supported student onboarding and front-desk operations in a fast-paced university environment.

Built a survey schema mapping service that ingests a survey template alongside sample responses and auto-generates a JSON field mapping, allowing downstream pipeline tasks to extract structured data from raw survey files regardless of format or structure.

Addressed a core data heterogeneity problem: school district surveys arrive in varying file formats and layouts, and the mapping service abstracts that complexity into a consistent, reusable schema contract.

Worked within InnovateK12's stakeholder analytics platform, which processes community survey data from K-12 districts to drive insights on student wellbeing, strategic planning, and community engagement.
Led backend development using CLEAN architecture, building RESTful APIs with ASP.NET, reducing integration time by 30%.
Designed and deployed an MS SQL database managing 1000+ vendor and expense records with secure access control.
Developed front-end features with JavaScript ES6 and integrated vendor management, invoice generation, and document signing, improving process efficiency by 25%.
Enabled secure file storage in Azure Blob Storage and integrated PDF generation and automated email features, cutting document turnaround time by 40%.

projects.title: Intelligent NPC: LLM-Driven Multi-Agent Simulation
projects.description: A Python framework that gives game NPCs autonomous, socially-aware behaviour using LLMs. Each NPC maintains a personal Belief Graph, a weighted knowledge graph where beliefs about other characters decay over time, weaken with each rumour hop, and strengthen through confirmation. A social-memory engine for game AI.
bullets: Each NPC holds a personal Belief Graph where beliefs decay over time, weaken with each gossip hop, and strengthen through confirmation, so rumours and direct witnesses affect behaviour differently.
Per-tick loop: witness enrichment → belief propagation (personality-filtered) → LLM decision call with each NPC's full context (goals, beliefs, nearby characters).
Fully scenario-agnostic: YAML files define worlds independently from the engine, enabling emergent social dynamics without any scripted logic.

projects.title: IntelliMove: Talent-to-Role Matching
projects.description: AI-powered system that evaluates employee skill profiles against job descriptions using multi-factor scoring that accounts for skill decay, confidence levels, partial category credit, and sentiment analysis from performance reviews.
bullets: Scoring across four dimensions: skill relevance with staleness decay, confidence aggregated from resumes and reviews, partial category credit, and sentiment modifiers from performance review text.
Used Claude (AWS Bedrock) for skill normalisation and hierarchical grouping, handling synonyms and skill variants automatically.
96.2% fit accuracy on a test dataset of 9 employees × 5 roles, correctly separating managerial vs. IC requirements.

projects.title: TenantLens: Rental Transparency Platform
projects.description: Renter-focused web application that combats rental fraud through community-driven property reviews, scam reporting, and document-verified residency badges.
bullets: Document-based residency verification with configurable address-matching thresholds; only renters with matching documentation receive a trusted 'verified stay' badge.
Production-grade security with Azure Key Vault and Managed Identity; no hardcoded credentials anywhere in the codebase.
Community-driven scam reporting builds a public record of fraudulent listings, giving future renters signal that anonymous review sites can't provide.

projects.title: Bus Headway: Real-Time Stream Processing
projects.description: Distributed streaming pipeline that monitors transit service intervals in real time, detecting bus bunching and service gaps to trigger live operational alerts.
bullets: Kafka + Spark Structured Streaming with stateful event-time aggregations and Kappa Architecture: no batch layer, continuous recalculation as position events arrive.
Full stack containerised in Docker Compose: Spark workers, Kafka brokers, Zookeeper, and MongoDB running as fault-tolerant services.
Real-time anomaly detection flags bunching as it forms; hourly MongoDB aggregations surface recurring problem routes for longer-term scheduling.

projects.title: ViGen: Story to Scene Video Pipeline
projects.description: Transforms a user-guided concept (tone, genre, topic, scene count) into a complete narrated video. Story generation, scene planning, video synthesis, and audio are all automated from start to finish.
bullets: LangChain multi-agent pipeline: StoryAgent scripts scenes, SceneAgent plans visuals, Amazon Nova Reel (Bedrock) generates video and Polly handles narration, all assembled into MP4 via FFmpeg.
Async generation across video and audio keeps latency low; assets staged in S3 enable partial restarts without regenerating the full video.
Automatic prompt softening logic rewrites requests likely to hit content filters before retrying, ensuring reliable output across diverse story themes.

projects.title: LLM-Agent: Modular AI Agent Framework
projects.description: LLM-agnostic agent framework for building reasoning agents with tools, memory, and RAG. Swap between Gemini, Ollama, or Groq with no code changes.
bullets: Provider abstraction layer supports Gemini, Ollama, and Groq behind one interface; swap models with a config change, no code changes.
Built-in tools: Python execution, Serper web search, scraping, and file I/O. RAG via FAISS + SentenceTransformers for persistent knowledge retrieval.
Deployed as a FastAPI service on Render with SQLite persistence; works cloud or fully offline via Ollama.

projects.title: Stock Vision: ML Stock Forecasting Platform
projects.description: Full-stack web app that forecasts stock prices using three ML models and Twitter sentiment analysis, delivering actionable BUY/SELL signals on a live dashboard of 20 major stocks.
bullets: Three models per stock (LSTM, Linear Regression, and ARIMA) with signals synthesised into a consensus BUY/SELL recommendation to reduce single-model bias.
Sentiment pipeline: snscrape + TextBlob + spaCy scores Twitter data and feeds a weighted modifier into the final score, catching momentum shifts price data misses.
React 18 frontend (Highcharts, Plotly, D3.js) over Flask, delivering 7-day forecasts for 20 stocks including TSLA, NVDA, AAPL, and AMZN.
